lede: A look at January 14 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including J. Bernlef and Leonardo Ortolani, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Elvis Presley
events.desc: Elvis Presley's concert Aloha from Hawaii is broadcast live via satellite, and sets the record as the most watched broadcast by an individual entertainer in television history.

events.title: USS Enterprise fire
events.desc: USS Enterprise fire: An accidental explosion aboard the USS Enterprise near Hawaii kills 28 people.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Japan begins Operation Ke, the successful operation to evacuate its forces from Guadalcanal during the Guadalcanal campaign.

events.title: Franklin D. Roosevelt
events.desc: World War II: Franklin D. Roosevelt and Winston Churchill begin the Casablanca Conference to discuss strategy and study the next phase of the war.

events.title: Queen Maud Land
events.desc: Norway claims Queen Maud Land in Antarctica.

events.title: Roald Amundsen
events.desc: Roald Amundsen's South Pole expedition makes landfall on the eastern edge of the Ross Ice Shelf.

events.title: 1907 Kingston earthquake
events.desc: An earthquake in Kingston, Jamaica kills more than 1,000 people.

events.title: Giacomo Puccini
events.desc: Giacomo Puccini's Tosca opens in Rome.

events.title: Napoleon III
events.desc: Napoleon III of France escapes an assassination attempt made by Felice Orsini and his accomplices in Paris.

events.title: Andrew III of Hungary
events.desc: Andrew III of Hungary dies, ending the Árpád dynasty in Hungary.

births.name: Leonardo Ortolani
births.line: Italian author and illustrator, created Rat-Man

deaths.text: Arfa Karim, Pakistani student and computer prodigy, youngest Microsoft Certified Professional in 2004
Lewis Carroll, English novelist, poet, and mathematician
Andrew III of Hungary, King of Hungary and Croatia from 1290 to 1301
